about.title: About
about.subtitle: A small helper for the big love you have for your pets.
about.appTagline: Everything about your pet's health in one place.
about.appDescription: Pawport combines a digital health passport and intelligent food analysis for dogs, cats and other pets. It helps you keep track of vaccinations, medications and vet visits — and make food decisions with confidence. So you can focus on what matters most: your pet.
about.privacyDescription: Access is protected by sign-in and all your pet records are private — only you can see them.

aiAnalysis.title: AI food analysis
aiAnalysis.description: Type or photograph the ingredients and in seconds you'll know what your pet is getting.

healthPassport.title: Digital health passport
healthPassport.description: Vaccinations, deworming, medications and vet visits — all clearly in one place.

diary.title: Health diary
diary.description: Record health incidents and track what works and what doesn't.

vetCard.title: Vet card
vetCard.description: A neat summary you can show or print at the vet.

profiles.title: Pet profiles
profiles.description: Dogs, cats and other pets — each with their own profile and allergy list.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about how Pawport works and what happens to your data.

items.a: Your pet profiles and health records are stored in a secure cloud database (Supabase) via our server. They are tied to your account — they are not stored only in the browser.
Only you. Every request is bound to your signed-in account and the database is configured so that no one can access your data without your login.

items.q: Can I access my data from multiple devices?
items.a: Yes. Since the data is in the cloud, after signing in you'll see it on your phone, tablet and computer — always up to date.
Communication uses encrypted HTTPS, access is only possible after sign-in, and database secret keys are exclusively on the server, never in the browser.

items.q: Are scans and photos I upload for recognition stored?
items.a: No. When you upload a photo of a passport or food packaging for AI recognition, it is only used to read the text — you get the result back, but the image itself is not stored on the server.

items.q: What about photos I attach directly to a record or episode?
items.a: Those yes — attachments you deliberately add to a record are saved as part of it, so you can see them later.
Text or photos are processed by AI services (OpenAI and Google Vision), which recognize the content and return the result. We only send what is needed for the evaluation.

items.q: Is the analysis result medical advice?
items.a: No. Results are informational and serve as a guide. For health decisions, always consult a veterinarian.
With email and password, or with a single click via your Google account.

items.q: I forgot my password, what now?
items.a: On the login page click "Forgot password?" and we'll send you an email to set a new one.
Yes. Create a profile for each pet — dogs, cats and other animals, each with their own allergies and records.

items.q: What happens when I delete a pet?
items.a: Along with the profile, all related records are deleted too (vaccinations, medications, episodes, expenses…). This action cannot be undone.

items.q: How do I delete my account and all my data?
items.a: In the side menu click "Delete account". Your account and all saved data will be permanently removed. This action is irreversible.
Yes, the app is fully responsive and you can add it to your home screen from the browser as an app.

hero.chip: For dogs, cats and other pets
hero.description: A digital health passport for dogs, cats and other pets. AI food analysis and a vet card all in one place. Never miss a vaccination, deworming or check-up.
hero.dogPhotoAlt: Golden retriever looking at the camera

howItWorks.title: 3 steps and your health passport is on your phone
howItWorks.subtitle: No complicated forms, no monthly fees. Get started in a few minutes.

steps.title: Create a pet profile
steps.text: Add name, breed, age, weight, allergies and health conditions. Takes 60 seconds.

steps.title: Scan the health passport
steps.text: AI extracts vaccinations, deworming and other records from a photo of the passport or a PDF.

steps.title: Vet card
steps.text: Before every vet visit, export a clear PDF card with everything that matters.

features.subtitle: Six features that together cover the entire health life of your pet.

items.title: Health passport
items.text: Vaccinations, deworming, ectoparasites and chronic diagnoses in one clear timeline.

items.title: AI food analysis
items.text: Enter ingredients or photograph the packaging — AI evaluates quality and allergens for your specific pet.

items.title: Reminders
items.text: The card always shows what's needed: overdue deworming, upcoming vaccination, check-up.

items.title: Vet card
items.text: A professional PDF document with everything — identity, diagnoses, vaccinations, history.

items.title: Can my pet eat…?
items.text: A quick AI question for one food item. Chocolate? Apple? You'll get a clear answer.

items.title: Health diary
items.text: Record health episodes and based on history see what worked and what didn't.

comparison.title: Paper passport vs Pawport
comparison.subtitle: Why switch from a worn paper passport to a digital health passport.
paperPoints: You have to carry it everywhere
pawportPoints: Always on your phone, never lost

Clear vet card with one click

cta.title: Start caring for your pet's health today
cta.subtitle: No registration, no fees. Just add a pet profile and you're ready to use the digital health passport.

appPreview.title: This is what your digital passport looks like
appPreview.subtitle: Works on desktop and mobile — all records synced, always with you.

photoWall.subtitle: Dogs, cats, rabbits, birds, reptiles and horses — Pawport handles a health card for every pet.

demo.title: Ask AI what your pet can eat
demo.subtitle: Type a food or ingredient — instantly see if it's safe for your dog, cat or other pet.
demo.continueBtn: Continue with real AI in the app

fallback.short: Without full AI I can't give a reliable answer.
fallback.explanation: This is just a demo with a limited list of foods. For accurate evaluation, use the real AI in the app.
warnings: Consult your vet before giving any new food

chocolate.short: No, chocolate is toxic to dogs.
chocolate.explanation: It contains theobromine, which is toxic to dogs and can cause vomiting, tremors, seizures or heart failure.

grapes.short: No, grapes can cause kidney failure.
grapes.explanation: Even a small amount of grapes or raisins can cause acute kidney failure in dogs.

onion.short: No, onions damage red blood cells.
onion.explanation: Onions, garlic and chives contain thiosulfates that break down red blood cells and can cause anaemia.

xylitol.explanation: The sweetener xylitol rapidly lowers blood sugar and can cause liver failure even in small doses.

carrot.short: Yes, carrots are an excellent snack.
carrot.explanation: Carrots are low in calories, contain fibre and beta-carotene. Raw carrots also help clean teeth.

apple.short: Yes, but remove the seeds and stem.
apple.explanation: Apples are healthy and tasty, but the seeds contain traces of cyanide. Only give the fleshy pieces.

banana.explanation: Rich in potassium and vitamins. Give only in moderate amounts due to high sugar content.

chicken bones.short: Depends on preparation — never give cooked.
chicken bones.explanation: Cooked bones splinter and can injure the digestive tract. Raw large bones are acceptable under supervision.
warnings: Cooked bones can injure the digestive tract
Only raw and larger bones under supervision

milk.explanation: Some dogs digest milk fine, others get diarrhoea. Try a small amount and monitor the reaction.

cheese.explanation: Hard cheeses low in lactose are OK as treats. Watch out for salt content.
warnings: Not for dogs with dairy allergies

avocado.explanation: Persin in avocado can cause digestive problems and vomiting. The pit is also a choking hazard.

salmon.short: Yes, but only cooked and boneless.
salmon.explanation: Salmon is an excellent source of omega-3 fatty acids. Raw salmon may contain parasites.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: We'll remind you when vaccinations, deworming, antiparasitic treatments, vet check-ups, or medication end dates are approaching.
notifications.emailNoEmail: Your account has no email — add one by signing in with email.
notifications.leadDaysNote: We'll also remind you on the due date and the day after.
notifications.minLeadDaysError: Keep at least one reminder interval.
notifications.pushComing: Push notifications to your phone are coming soon.
